programmes.show_description: Alf, the Reads and the Rawlins get involved in an argument about their elected representatives. S6 E4: Wedgie Ben
With Suzi Quatro and Joe Jackson. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
While catching up on some downtime in the Bahamas, Simon becomes involved in the death of his friend's sister after she is the target of a blackmail plot. S3 E2: Lida

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
After WWII, Britain rushed the Comet jetliner into service. Mysterious crashes led to tests that reshaped aircraft design forever. Narrated by Robert Powell.
This unique series narrated by Sir Tony Robinson uses home movie archive footage to reveal life in 20th-century Britain as never seen before - this episode features footage from the 1960s. S1 E4
The American army was struggling to engage an elusive enemy; learn about the Viet Cong's secret methods to hinder America's attempts to seal off South Vietnam. S2 E9: Vietnam - Hidden in Plain Sight
The F-4 Phantom II is an American tandem two-seat, twin-engine, long-range supersonic jet interceptor and fighter-bomber originally developed by McDonnell Aircraft for the United States Navy. S3 E5
The 1920s and 1930s were uncertain times for the RAF and all other armed services; with peace came cuts in spending on new aircraft development. Ep2: Under Distant Skies
When American forces began the reconquest of the Philippine islands in 1944, they faced a terrifying weapon - the Kamikaze suicide attack. Ep9: The Kamikazes
The campaign in Tunisia in World War Two saw the battles that finally ended the brutal war in the desert. We look at the weapons used and tactics and tell the story of the battle itself.
The story of Colonel Count Claus von Stauffenberg and the other German officers who tried to kill Adolf Hitler in a bomb plot of July 1944. Ep3: The Man Who Stood Up To Hitler
The machine gun did not dominate the battlefield of WWII - unlike WWI - but it remained a highly potent weapon including being incorporated into the wings of fighter planes. Ep9: The Machine Gun
We experience the event that changed the war's course and feel the momentum behind the attack that provoked the world's greatest superpower to join the Allied forces. Ep4: Pearl Harbor
A world war is a patchwork of actions, land, sea and air; Japan gathers naval strength for an attack on Midway Island as they attempt to lure the United States into a trap. Ep6: Fragmentation
Priceless art, personal assets and national treasures were an extraordinary amount of loot prized by Hitler as much as territory - we discover the organised plunder of these riches. S1 E5: Nazi Gold
The Kirbridge marriage is in trouble as Elizabeth is feeling unwanted by Lawrence and he refuses to discuss her want to start a family - does the poet have other interests? S2 E3: Married Love
Arthur puts his savings into an old safe in the lock-up, but his nest egg is put in danger when an old acquaintance arrives from Marbella. S7 E5: Fiddler on the Hoof
Dempsey and Makepeace find themselves in grave danger when an injured bare-knuckle boxer sparks off an investigation into counterfeiters. S3 E3: The Prizefighter
When animal rights activists threaten a series of attacks in London and place the daughter of a laboratory owner in hospital, Gene is determined to track them down. S2 E3
It's the day of Minnie's mother's funeral. After the ceremony, Alf finds he has a shocking cough. S6 E5: The Wake
With The Police. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
A visiting diplomat is of interest to several people because of her pearl necklace. S3 E3: Jeannine

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
By 1915 trench warfare had stalled, Naval and amphibious plans had failed disastrously, revealing why the Dardanelles campaign collapsed. Narrated by Robert Powell.
We compare the elite teams at the frontlines of the fight against terror against past battles comparing American riflemen with British line soldiers and the story of American sniper Timothy Murphy.
As the armies of World War I struggled under a barrage of terrifying weapons, the German government turned to covert operations to break the stalemate. S2 E10: WW1 - Germany's Secret Gambles
A look at London's first 1,000 years includes treasures unearthed by Crossrail, the ruins of Roman Londinium, handwritten messages dating back 2,000 years and the first London Bridge. Ep1
The RAF had aircraft that could escort bombers to targets deep inside Germany and back by the end of World War One, and the Hawker Hunter and F-86 Sabre took the RAF supersonic. Ep3: A Vicious Sting
The Royal Navy fought a long campaign against the German U-boats and surface ships in the Atlantic; its Pacific Fleet helped win victory over Japan. Ep10: The Royal Navy

We examine the course of savage fighting in the Balkans in the latter half of the conflict in World War 2 where after the uprising by the Romanians, the Soviet army moved swiftly to capture Bucharest.
The story of the men that cracked the Nazis' Enigma code, from Polish mathematicians to the establishment at Bletchley Park and the work of Alan Turing. Ep4: The Men Who Cracked Enigma
Fast patrol boats armed with torpedoes and machine guns were the marine raiders of World War II; this programme looks at some of their most spectacular raids. Ep10: Fast Attack Boats
Devastation touches the civilians of Germany; with the US as a newly-engaged ally, Western forces bring the war to the Axis front but at extreme costs. Ep5: Bombing The Reich
Battles in WWII occur in freezing temperatures, humid jungles, snowy hills and arid deserts; Allied and Japanese forces struggle for control of Port Moresby. Ep7: Multidimensional Warfare
We take a look at one of WWII's most significant theatres of warfare, in which Germany's U-boat fleet relentlessly attacked Britain's supply lines. S1 E6: Battle of the Atlantic
Elizabeth and Lawrence's marriage appears to have ended as it becomes apparent that her visit for a simple tea has more to it than first meets the eye. S2 E4: Whom God Hath Joined
A local property developer offers to buy Arthur's lock-up and car lot, but Terry suspects that the deal is bent and turns to DS Jones for help. S7 E6: The Wrong Goodbye
Dempsey and Makepeace pose as Middle East terrorists to find an arms dealer, and Dempsey undertakes a routine psychological assessment. S3 E4: Extreme Prejudice
A visitor from Gene Hunt's old stomping ground of Manchester has a surprise in store, and Alex is desperate to get to the bottom of the corruption she believes is in the force. S2 E4
